HIGH COURT OF AUSTRALIA

Smith v Jones [2026] HCA 15

KIEFEL CJ, GAGELER, GORDON, STEWARD AND GLEESON JJ

23 March 2026

JUDGMENT

This appeal concerns the constitutional validity of the proposed legislation. The appellant challenges the decision of the Full Court of the Federal Court on the ground that the legislation impermissibly confers judicial power on a non-judicial body. The central question is whether the functions conferred are inherently judicial in character.

For the reasons that follow, the appeal should be allowed. The legislation is invalid to the extent that it purports to confer judicial power contrary to Chapter III of the Constitution.
